findings.title: Veeam Backup & Replication — Five Critical/High CVEs Allow Authenticated RCE and Credential Theft
findings.affected_technology: Veeam Backup & Replication (all versions prior to latest fixed build per KB4831)
findings.summary: Veeam patched 5 high/critical flaws on March 12: two CVSS 9.9 RCE bugs (domain user / Backup Viewer → code execution on Backup Server), one CVSS 9.1 HA RCE, a CVSS 8.8 local privilege escalation, and CVSS 7.7 SSH credential extraction. No PoC yet, but Veeam is ransomware groups' top backup-destruction target (Akira, Fog).

findings.title: "Poisoned Typeface" — Font-Rendering PoC Hides Malicious Commands from All Major AI Assistants
findings.cvss: N/A (rejected as out-of-scope by all vendors except Microsoft, who fully patched Copilot)
findings.affected_technology: AI browser assistants: ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok, Perplexity, Leo, Dia, Fellou, Genspark (Microsoft Copilot patched)
findings.summary: LayerX PoC uses custom web fonts with glyph substitution to split DOM from rendered content: AI assistant reads safe text, browser shows a reverse-shell command, AI approves it as safe. Tested against 11 assistants in Dec 2025 — all failed. Live PoC still up. Microsoft alone patched; all others declined.

findings.title: LeakNet Ransomware Combines ClickFix Social Engineering with Deno Runtime Loader for Stealthy Compromise
findings.affected_technology: Enterprise Windows environments susceptible to ClickFix social engineering
findings.summary: LeakNet RaaS uses ClickFix (fake CAPTCHA/browser-fix tricking users to paste PowerShell) for initial access, then deploys a loader built on the Deno JS runtime — signed legitimate Deno binaries evade EDR allowlisting — combining a rapidly spreading social-engineering technique with a living-off-trusted-binaries evasion.

findings.title: Starkiller PhaaS Uses Live AitM Headless-Chrome Reverse Proxy to Bypass MFA and Harvest Session Tokens
findings.affected_technology: Any MFA-protected web app (Microsoft 365 primary target; any brand configurable via dashboard)
findings.summary: Starkiller (Jinkusu group) runs headless Chrome in Docker as a live AitM reverse proxy — victim authenticates through attacker infrastructure against the real site in real time, capturing all credentials, OTPs, and session tokens; no static templates to fingerprint; SMS/TOTP MFA fully bypassed; only FIDO2 hardware keys resist.

findings.title: CVE-2026-0628 "Glic Jack" — Chrome Gemini Side Panel Lets Malicious Extensions Access Files, Camera, and Mic
findings.summary: Unit 42 published full disclosure of CVE-2026-0628: insufficient WebView policy in Chrome's Gemini side panel (chrome://glic) lets a malicious extension with basic permissions inject into a high-privilege context, gaining access to local files, camera, microphone, and arbitrary website screenshots. Patched Chrome 143 Jan 2026.

findings.title: CVE-2026-29000 — pac4j JWT Auth Bypass via alg=none PlainJWT in JWE — Public PoC Available
findings.cvss: Critical (maximum severity per vendor; NVD score pending)
findings.affected_technology: pac4j-jwt prior to versions 4.5.9 / 5.7.9 / 6.3.3 (used in Java frameworks incl. Apache Shiro, Spring)
findings.summary: Unauthenticated attacker knowing only the server's RSA public key (often at JWKS endpoint) can forge JWEs with a PlainJWT inner token; logic error skips signature verification when SignedJWT is null, enabling full user impersonation including admins. Public PoC exists. Fix: upgrade pac4j-jwt to 4.5.9+ / 5.7.9+ / 6.3.3+.

findings.title: Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.6 Autonomously Discovers 22 Firefox Bugs Including CVSS 9.8 JIT Flaw CVE-2026-2796
findings.cvss: 9.8 (CVE-2026-2796); plus 14 high and 7 moderate severity bugs all patched in Firefox 148
findings.summary: Claude Opus 4.6 scanned ~6,000 Firefox C++ files in 2 weeks in Jan 2026, finding 22 bugs (14 high); detected CVSS 9.8 WebAssembly JIT use-after-free in 20 min; ~$4K API spend yielded working exploits for 2 bugs; signals AI-driven vuln discovery now accessible at four-digit cost with narrowing gap to weaponization. All patched in Firefox 148.

findings.title: Pingora OSS HTTP/1 Request Smuggling — Three CVEs in Cloudflare's Open-Source Proxy (Fixed in 0.8.0)
findings.affected_technology: Cloudflare Pingora OSS prior to version 0.8.0 (Cloudflare production infra is NOT affected)
findings.summary: Three HTTP/1 request smuggling flaws in standalone Pingora OSS deployments enable cache poisoning, cross-user session hijacking, and proxy security-control bypass. Non-public PoC provided during responsible disclosure. Fix in Pingora 0.8.0. Increasingly popular as NGINX/HAProxy alternative.

findings.title: Elevated Cyber Risk from Iran/Israel Conflict: BadeSaba App Compromised, 149 Hacktivist DDoS Attacks in 48 Hours
findings.affected_technology: Critical infrastructure, financial services, government, defense, energy (global — elevated for any org with perceived conflict linkage)
findings.summary: FortiGuard warns no large-scale confirmed Iranian retaliation yet but: 149 hacktivist DDoS attacks hit 110 orgs in 16 countries in 48 hrs; BadeSaba calendar app push-notification backend compromised (implies pre-staging); Iran historically activates pre-staged access weeks post-trigger — treat current quiet as defensive window, not safety signal.

noted.product: Oracle Edge Cloud Infrastructure Designer and Visualisation Toolkit v0.3.0
noted.summary: CVSS 9.8 unauthenticated network RCE via HTTP; niche product but critical severity — monitor for PoC and active exploitation campaigns.
MFA bypass allowing attacker with valid credentials to skip multi-factor authentication; fixed in GLPI 11.0.6; CVSS 6.5; low exploitation risk currently.

noted.product: Cloud Foundry CAPI Release v1.226.0 and below
noted.summary: Unprotected internal endpoints allow droplet replacement by any user bypassing the firewall; CVSS 7.5 High; relevant to enterprise PaaS on Cloud Foundry.
Buffer overflow on TLS cookies over 256B (CVE-2026-27459) and connection acceptance when servername callback raises exception (CVE-2026-27448); both fixed in pyOpenSSL 26.0.0.

noted.product: OpenClaw AI — Exposed Admin Interface and ClawHub Supply Chain (Krebs/DVULN Research)
noted.summary: Krebs feature on DVULN research: hundreds of internet-exposed OpenClaw admin interfaces leaking all credentials (API keys, OAuth tokens, bot tokens); new scope from 2026-03-01 ClawJacked (WebSocket) finding; no CVE yet — monitor for PoC and CVE assignment.

noted.product: GTIG AI Threat Tracker — Q4 2025 Adversarial AI (Google/Mandiant)
noted.summary: DPRK/Iran/PRC/Russia using LLMs for phishing and recon; HONESTCUE malware calls Gemini API for dynamic second-stage payload generation; Xanthorox underground jailbreak service uses open-source MCP servers; model extraction distillation attacks rising from private sector worldwide.
